narrative:
Matheus Cunha is a Centre-Forward for Brazil. His overall rating of 7.13/10 places him in the top 5% of centre-forwards at this tournament. This rating has a range of 6.75–7.52, reflecting the match sample size, with 40% derived from this World Cup.

Cunha demonstrates clinical finishing at this World Cup, scoring 0.922 goals per 90 minutes from an xG of 0.438 per 90 minutes. He also contributes defensively, making 1.843 tackles and 1.843 clearances per 90 minutes. However, his playmaking is limited, with only 0.027 expected assists and 0.307 key passes per 90 minutes.

Matheus Cunha is a vital goal threat for Brazil, having played 293 minutes at this World Cup. Scouts should continue to monitor his exceptional finishing ability and consistent defensive work rate. His high shot volume of 3.072 per 90 minutes and clinical edge will be crucial in upcoming games.
